specification.topic-statement: The input action dispatcher routes user input events to specialized handlers and manages scroll detection, completion state, and interrupt requests within the REPL.

scope.in-scope: Dispatching all 29 input action types to their respective handlers, scroll detection interception and buffered arrow flush, escape-key interrupt handling, and tool process interrupt with SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation.
scope.boundary: Text editing, completion cycling, history browsing, viewport scrolling, agent navigation, and LLM submission are handled by separate modules. This topic dispatches to them and propagates any errors they return.
Raw bytes are parsed into input actions externally. This topic receives fully parsed actions.

behavior.scroll-detection-pre-processing:
Arrow-up and arrow-down events are intercepted before main dispatch and passed to the scroll detector state machine along with the current CLOCK_MONOTONIC timestamp. The detector may: - Buffer the event (returning a signal that the caller is done — no further processing for this action) - Absorb the event as part of a mouse-scroll burst (same signal) - Classify it as a scroll-up or scroll-down and execute the corresponding viewport scroll action - Pass it through as a plain arrow key (continue to main dispatch)
If the scroll detector is absent, arrow events fall through to main dispatch immediately.
behavior.pending-arrow-flush: When any event other than arrow-up, arrow-down, or unknown arrives, the scroll detector's pending buffered arrow (if any) is flushed first. A flushed arrow-up triggers the arrow-up viewport action; a flushed arrow-down triggers the arrow-down viewport action. This occurs before the incoming event is processed.
behavior.char-input: If a completion is active and the character is a space, the completion is committed. Otherwise: any active history browsing is stopped, the viewport offset is reset to zero, the codepoint is inserted into the input buffer, and the completion context is updated with the new text.
behavior.newline: Delegated to the LLM action handler, which determines whether the input is a slash command or a regular user message and acts accordingly.
behavior.insert-newline: The viewport offset is reset to zero and a literal newline is inserted into the input buffer without submitting.
behavior.backspace-and-delete: The viewport offset is reset to zero, the character before or after the cursor is removed, and the completion context is updated.
behavior.arrow-left-and-right: Any active completion is dismissed. The viewport offset is reset to zero. The cursor moves one position in the input buffer.
behavior.cursor-control-keys: Ctrl+A, Ctrl+E, Ctrl+K, Ctrl+U, and Ctrl+W all reset the viewport offset to zero and then perform their respective input buffer operations: move to line start, move to line end, kill to line end, kill entire line, and delete word backward.
behavior.history-navigation: Ctrl+P and Ctrl+N return success immediately with no action. These keys are reserved for future reverse-search functionality.
behavior.ctrl-c: An interrupt request is submitted for any in-flight operation, then the REPL quit flag is set to trigger graceful shutdown.
behavior.tab: Delegated to the completion handler, which triggers or cycles completions.
behavior.escape: The current agent state is read atomically. If the agent is in WAITING_FOR_LLM or EXECUTING_TOOL state, an interrupt request is submitted. If the agent is idle and a completion is active with an original input saved, the input buffer is restored to that original text before the completion is dismissed.
behavior.viewport-scrolling: Arrow-up and arrow-down (after scroll detection pass-through), page-up, page-down, scroll-up, and scroll-down all delegate to the viewport action handlers.
behavior.agent-navigation: Navigation-prev-sibling, navigation-next-sibling, navigation-parent, and navigation-child delegate to the agent tree navigation system.
behavior.unknown-input: Returns success immediately with no action.
behavior.interrupt-request:
Called from Ctrl+C and the escape key handler. The agent state is read atomically. If the agent is idle, nothing happens. Otherwise, the interrupt-requested flag is set on the agent. Then:
If the agent is in WAITING_FOR_LLM state and the provider has a cancel operation, that operation is invoked to abort the in-flight HTTP stream.
If the agent is in EXECUTING_TOOL state and a child process PID is recorded, SIGTERM is sent to the entire process group. The system then polls every 100 milliseconds (up to 2000 milliseconds total) using a non-blocking wait. If the process has not exited after 2000 milliseconds, SIGKILL is sent.
behavior.multiline-scrollback-append: A helper operation splits a multi-line string by newline characters and appends each resulting segment to the scrollback as a separate line. Empty segments at boundaries are included when they appear before the end of the string.
